# Skill manifest lint: perFilePayloadBudget primitive (narrowing of #11320 AC1-3 + AC6-9; AC4-5 stay parented to #11320)

## Context

Narrowing-carve-out of [#11320](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11320) per [PR #11324](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11324) Cycle 1 review by @neo-gpt (close-target audit RA1).

PR #11324 ships the **per-file byte cap primitive** (#11320 AC1-3 + AC6-9) but **defers** the **section-trigger parser** (#11320 AC4) and **trigger-frequency × size heuristic** (#11320 AC5) — those require Sub-B+ migration substrate to exist before the parser has anything to parse + the heuristic has anything to heurise against.

Per GPT's prescription:
> *"retarget the PR to a narrower non-epic ticket for the per-file cap primitive and keep #11320 open for parser/heuristic work"*

This is that narrower ticket. PR #11324 retargets `Resolves #11320` → `Resolves #THIS`; #11320 stays OPEN for AC4-5 follow-up implementation under a separate PR after Sub-B+ migrations land.

## The Fix (as shipped in PR #11324)

### Schema extension (`skills.manifest.schema.json` + `skills.manifest.json`)

- New optional `perFilePayloadBudget` field in `defaults` block + per-skill `$defs/skill` properties
- Positive integer when present; **omit to disable** per-file enforcement (canonical nullable-contract semantics)
- `defaults.perFilePayloadBudget = 25000` (25 KB empirical floor for clean skills)
- Per-skill overrides for migration-period monoliths: `pr-review.perFilePayloadBudget = 66000`, `pull-request.perFilePayloadBudget = 38000` (tighten via Sub-B+ migrations)

### Lint logic extension (`ai/scripts/lint-skill-manifest.mjs`)

- New `payloadFileSizes()` walker (returns `{path, bytes}` per file under `references/`)
- New pure exported function `checkPerFileBudgets(files, perFileBudget)` returning error array (testable + reusable)
- Main `lint()` cascades `skill.perFilePayloadBudget ?? defaults.perFilePayloadBudget` and fails any file exceeding the cap with Required-Action template directing to `skill-authoring-guide.md` Map vs World Atlas extraction discipline

### `/create-skill` discipline update (`skill-authoring-guide.md`)

- Appended "Recursive Application: Workflow Files Are Also Maps" subsection documenting canonical `<!-- trigger: [condition] → read [sub-rule.md] -->` syntax + mechanical-enforcement cross-reference + empirical precedents

### Tests (`test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/lintSkillManifest.spec.mjs`)

- Schema accepts `perFilePayloadBudget` as optional `defaults` field
- Schema accepts `perFilePayloadBudget` as optional per-skill override
- Validator rejects non-positive `perFilePayloadBudget` (zero, negative)
- Backwards-compat: manifest without `perFilePayloadBudget` passes cleanly
- **NEW (per GPT RA2):** `checkPerFileBudgets` returns error for over-budget file + correct error message shape
- **NEW (per GPT RA2):** `checkPerFileBudgets` returns no errors when all files under budget
- **NEW (per GPT RA3 nullable-contract):** `checkPerFileBudgets` treats null/undefined/non-positive budget as disabled (omit-to-disable rule)

13/13 tests pass locally.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] **AC1: Schema field added** — `perFilePayloadBudget` in defaults + per-skill (optional; `omit-to-disable` semantics)
- [x] **AC2: Manifest populated** — `defaults.perFilePayloadBudget = 25000` + 2 monolith overrides (pr-review 66000, pull-request 38000)
- [x] **AC3: Lint walks per-file** — `payloadFileSizes()` walker + `perFilePayloadBudget` cascade
- [x] **AC4: Pure exported `checkPerFileBudgets` function** — refactored from inline lint loop for testability + future reuse by sub-rule-extraction tooling
- [x] **AC5: `/create-skill` discipline updated** — `skill-authoring-guide.md` "Recursive Application" subsection
- [x] **AC6: No workflow migrations** — PR diff touches manifest + schema + lint script + skill-authoring-guide + tests ONLY (one-skill-per-PR migration discipline preserved for Sub-B+)
- [x] **AC7: Lint backwards-compatible** — Existing manifests without `perFilePayloadBudget` pass cleanly
- [x] **AC8: Nullable contract clarified** — "omit to disable; positive integer when present" is the canonical semantics (matches implementation; explicit `null` rejected by schema validator)
- [x] **AC9: Test coverage** — 13 specs cover all v1 behaviors including over-budget negative path + omit-to-disable contract
- [x] **AC10: `/turn-memory-pre-flight` retrospective** — skill-authoring-guide.md mutation correctly placed in `/create-skill` payload (conditionally-loaded only when `/create-skill` fires; not always-loaded)

## Out of Scope (explicit — stays in #11320 for separate implementation)

- **Section-trigger parser** (#11320 AC4 — `<!-- trigger: ... → ... -->` HTML-comment regex parser) — load-bearing once Sub-B+ migrations declare triggers
- **Trigger-frequency × size heuristic** (#11320 AC5 — rare-trigger pattern matcher + extraction-candidate flagging) — load-bearing once trigger declarations exist
- Tests for parser/heuristic paths (#11320 AC9 subset)

These stay parented to #11320 and ship in a follow-up PR after Sub-B+ migrations introduce trigger declarations to validate against.

- **Source ticket (retained for AC4-5):** [#11320](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11320)

- **Implementation PR:** [#11324](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11324)

- 2026-05-13T21:03:45Z @tobiu referenced in commit `d2c904b` - "feat(skills): add perFilePayloadBudget primitive (#11332) (#11324)

* feat(skills): add perFilePayloadBudget for recursive Map vs World Atlas enforcement (#11320)

Sub-A of Epic #11319 (Discussion #11314 graduation). Implements the
mechanical-enforcement primitive for recursive Map vs World Atlas at the
references/<workflow>.md tier:

- Schema: new optional `perFilePayloadBudget` field in defaults block
  AND per-skill entry (positive integer, omit to disable per-file
  enforcement).
- Manifest: defaults.perFilePayloadBudget = 25000 (25 KB empirical floor
  for clean skills). Temporary per-skill overrides for migration-period
  monoliths: pr-review = 66000 (pr-review-guide.md @ 57,388 B);
  pull-request = 38000 (pull-request-workflow.md @ 33,463 B). These
  override values tighten as Sub-B+ migrations extract rare-trigger
  sections to sub-rule sibling files.
- Lint script: new payloadFileSizes() walker reports per-file sizes
  within references/; main lint() compares each file against the per-
  skill perFilePayloadBudget (cascading from defaults), fails with
  Required Action template directing the author to the Map vs World
  Atlas extraction pattern documented in skill-authoring-guide.md.
- Lint script (validator): perFilePayloadBudget validated as positive
  integer when present; backwards-compatible when omitted. Unsupported-
  key checks tightened to use schema.properties keys instead of
  schema.required so optional fields don't false-fail.
- /create-skill discipline: skill-authoring-guide.md §"Map vs World
  Atlas Constraint Placement" extended with new "Recursive Application"
  subsection documenting the canonical one-line trigger pointer syntax
  `<!-- trigger: [condition] → read [sub-rule.md] -->` and the
  empirical-floor + temporary-override calibration model.
- Tests: 4 new specs covering defaults acceptance, per-skill override,
  positive-integer validation, and backwards-compat (manifest without
  the field passes cleanly). All 10 tests in
  lintSkillManifest.spec.mjs pass in 2.5s.

NO workflow migrations in this PR. Migration of pr-review-guide.md
and pull-request-workflow.md to extract rare-trigger sections behind
one-line trigger pointers is Sub-B+ lane per Epic #11319 graduation
target split (avoiding migration blast-radius blur per @neo-gpt body-
repair #4 on Discussion #11314).

Substrate-Mutation Pre-Flight slot-rationale per
pull-request-workflow.md §1.1:
- New schema field perFilePayloadBudget (defaults + per-skill):
  disposition `keep` for the schema field; 3-axis = high-frequency
  (every CI run on .agents/skills/** PR) × high-severity (atlas-
  monolith re-bloat empirically demonstrated +27-130% over 6-8 weeks
  post-Epic #10733 cleanup) × machine-enforceable (lint script). Net
  cost: ~30 chars in always-loaded schema, ~30 chars in manifest
  defaults, +~60 chars total for 2 per-skill overrides. Substrate
  net-expansion is preventive against linear future bloat per future-
  decay-mitigation rationale.
- skill-authoring-guide.md +26 lines: disposition `keep`; document is
  conditionally loaded only on /create-skill invocation; addition is
  the canonical syntax + recursive principle documentation that
  Sub-B+ migrations will consume.
- lint-skill-manifest.mjs +33 lines: disposition `keep`; mechanically
  enforces the principle.

Resolves Sub-A AC1-3, AC6-10 of Epic #11319. Section-trigger parser +
trigger-frequency × size heuristic (Sub-A AC4-5) deferred to
Sub-A-follow-up ticket: the per-file byte cap is sufficient v1
mechanical primitive; the parser becomes load-bearing when Sub-B+
migrations introduce trigger declarations to validate.
